description: Public opinion regarding the federal intervention and state accountability following the lead contamination of Flint's water supply in Michigan. The crisis, which began in 2014, reached a national boiling point in early 2016 as political pressure mounted for Governor Rick Snyder to resign.

key_events.description: President Obama declares a federal state of emergency in Flint.
Governor Snyder gives a public apology during the State of the State address.
Congressional hearings begin with testimony from EPA and state officials.
Formal criminal charges are announced against state-level health and environmental officials.

calibration_notes: Useful for measuring the shift from local apathy to intense national demand for institutional accountability during a public health disaster.
